# UC-11.2.5 · Meet Quality Monitoring

## Description

Poor meeting quality impacts productivity and user satisfaction. Monitoring enables network/infrastructure optimization.

## Value

Poor meeting quality impacts productivity and user satisfaction. Monitoring enables network/infrastructure optimization.

## Implementation

Ingest Meet quality data. Track jitter, latency, and packet loss per meeting. Alert on recurring poor quality for specific users or locations. Correlate with network performance data.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Google Meet quality logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest Meet quality data. Track jitter, latency, and packet loss per meeting. Alert on recurring poor quality for specific users or locations. Correlate with network performance data.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:meet"
| where video_recv_jitter_ms > 30 OR audio_recv_jitter_ms > 30
| stats count, avg(video_recv_jitter_ms) as avg_jitter by organizer_email, meeting_code
```

Understanding this SPL

**Meet Quality Monitoring** — Poor meeting quality impacts productivity and user satisfaction. Monitoring enables network/infrastructure optimization.

Documented **Data sources**: Google Meet quality logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:meet. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:meet". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where video_recv_jitter_ms > 30 OR audio_recv_jitter_ms > 30` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by organizer_email, meeting_code** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (poor quality meetings), Line chart (quality metrics trend), Bar chart (issues by location).

## SPL

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:meet"
| where video_recv_jitter_ms > 30 OR audio_recv_jitter_ms > 30
| stats count, avg(video_recv_jitter_ms) as avg_jitter by organizer_email, meeting_code
```

## Visualization

Table (poor quality meetings), Line chart (quality metrics trend), Bar chart (issues by location).
